MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library

  • Release Notes
MP4

Overview

mw_mp4 module creates MP4/MOV files, which helps you convert the hardware-encoded H264/H265 videos and AAC/ADTS audios into MP4 or MOV files for playback. For more information about the MP4 file format, refer to the following standards documents: ISO/IEC 14496-12:2012 Information technology — Coding of audio-visual objects — Part 12: ISO base media file format. QuickTime movie files (.mov) is an extensible multimedia framework developed by Apple Inc.

Besides creating the MP4 file, mw_mp4 also supports the following features:

  • Adding closed captions to the MP4/MOV files.
  • Repairing the MP4/MOV files accidentally corrupted during recording.

The mw_mp4.h file contains the definition of data structures and functions.

Key Features

  • Recording MP4/MOV Files

    mw_mp4 can convert video and audio streams in the following formats into MP4/MOV files.

    • H.264 or H.265 (HEVC) video tracks, which you can obtain from encoders such as ffmpeg or the GPU Hardware Encoding.
    • AAC/ADTS audio tracks, which you can obtain from encoders such as ffmpeg.
    • CC608 or CC708 captions. For more information about Closed Caption, refer to Closed Caption.

  • Repairing MP4/MOV Files

    Sometimes, the recorded MP4/MOV files may be damaged due to power outage or system crash, and cannot playback. The corrupted files can be fixed by referring to the Mp4Repair C++ example.

Examples

  1. Mp4Repair C++ demonstrates how to fix the corrupted MP4/MOV files.
  2. AVCapture C++ demonstrates how to record MP4 files.
  3. HDRCapture C++ demonstrates how to record HDR MP4 files.
  4. MWCCPlayer_DRLib C++ demonstrates how to record and save videos with closed captions as MP4 files.
